heading_content_part: Privacy PolicySkip to main contentSearch This BlogRamsonTechPrivacy PolicyRamsonTech(“Service”, “RamsonTech”, "we,""us," or "our") collects information from you in order toprovide better service and a better user experience.Your privacy is a top priority to us, and thisPrivacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your information.This does not cover any collection, use ordisclosure by third parties through any of our applications, products or
services Web sites that we do not control or own and any third party featuresor services made available via an Application.

decoder_heading: What information we use and how we use it:
ident_heading: What information we use and how we use it:
heading_content_part: What information we use and how we use it:
1.  Read your contactsRamsonTechdoes not save or upload your contacts. Permissionto access contact information is used when you search contacts inRamsonTechsearch bar.
2.  Take pictures and videosThis permission allowsRamsonTechto useyour device’s camera to take photos / videos and turn ON/OFF Camera Flash. Wedo not save or upload your photos/videos.

3.  Approximate location (network-based)This permission allowsRamsonTechtoidentify and display your location on map or apps installed by anonymoussurrounding users and to recommend popular apps based on users’ location. Thisinformation is untraceable.

4.  Read phone status and identityThis permission allowsRamsonTechtorecognize an incoming/outgoing call and to switch between the User Systeminterface and call interface or announces/shows the caller name while some oneis calling, you will identify it without looking to your smart phone.

decoder_heading: 5. Read your text messages (SMS or MMS)
ident_heading: 5.  Read your text messages (SMS or MMS
heading_content_part: 5.  Read your text messages (SMS or MMS)We do not save or upload your messages. We usethis permission to read your text messages when you search messages in searchbar or announce the sender name while some one is send you a message, you willidentify it without looking to your smart phone.

decoder_heading: 6. Modify or delete the contents of your USB storage
ident_heading:
6.  Modify or delete the contents of your
USB storag
heading_content_part: 6.  Modify or delete the contents of yourUSB storageThis permission allowsRamsonTechtodelete app data (image, text, audio etc..) stored on their SD card.

7.  Find accounts on the devicesRamsonTechdoes not recognize or save your accountinformation for any programs.RamsonTechonly detects whether users havea Google account linked with the device which helps us confirm the state ofGoogle Service and provide users with appropriate application download andupdate methods.

decoder_heading: 8. Read calendar events plus confidential information
ident_heading:
8.  Read calendar events plus confidential
informatio
heading_content_part: 8.  Read calendar events plus confidentialinformationThis information is used only in theRamsonTechto remind you of your latest calendar events. We do not save or upload anyof your calendar information.

decoder_heading: 9. Read the contents of your USB storage
ident_heading: 9.  Read the contents of your USB storag
heading_content_part: 9.  Read the contents of your USB storageThis permission allowsRamsonTechto viewapp data (image, text, audio etc..) stored on their SD card.
10.  Read Google service configurationThis information is used to acquire advertisingID.RamsonTechprovide users with better advertising service by usingsuch anonymous ID.
11.  Change network connectivityThis permission is used inRamsonTechsettingsand notification toolbar, in order to change/check network connectivity.
12.  Connect and disconnect from Wi-FiThis permission is used inRamsonTechsettingsand notification toolbar in order to connect and disconnect from Wi-Fi.
13.  Set an alarmThis permission allows users to access thedevice's alarm throughRamsonTechclock widget.
14.  Full network accessThis permission is used to access the device’snetwork for certain functions including receiving update notifications oraccessing app classification labels.
15.  View network connectionsThis permission is used inRamsonTechsettingsand notification toolbar, in order to view network connections.
16.  View Wi-Fi connectionsThis permission is used inRamsonTechsettingsand notification toolbar, in order to view Wi-Fi connections.
17.  Access Bluetooth settingsThis permission is used inRamsonTechsettings,in order to turn on and off the Bluetooth.
18.  Pair with Bluetooth devicesThis permission is used inRamsonTechsettings,in order to pair with Bluetooth devices.
19.  Close other appsThis permission is used inRamsonTechBoostin order to turn off the back-end apps and make the phone run faster.
20.  Retrieve running appsThis permission is used inRamsonTechBoostin order to view the running apps.
21.  Run as startupRamsonTechwill run as startup to provide users with alauncher service.
22.  Control vibrationThis permission allowsRamsonTechto makethe phone vibrate once after users set the device on vibrate, confirming thesetting has been turned on.
23.  Adjust your wallpaper sizeThis permission allows users to cropRamsonTechwallpaper to the desired size.
24.  Set wallpaperThis permission allows users to set a selectedRamsonTechimage as their wallpaper.

25.  Photos/Media/FilesModify or delete the contents of your USB storageTest access to protected storage When user recommends friends to downloadRamsonTechby sharing to social network, we need to save an introduction picture to beshared on SD card. We won’t access other file on SD card.
26.  Device & app historyRetrieve running appsWe use this information to boost your Android phoneeffectively, clean your device's RAM and make your phone speed up in depth.
27.  Measure app storage spaceThis permission is used to acquire the amount ofstorage space used by an application.
28.  Modify system settingsThis permission is used inRamsonTechsettings,in order to switch or adjust ringtone, vibration and brightness level of thescreen.
29.  Read Home settings and shortcutsThis permission is used to acquire the user’sHome settings and shortcuts, in order to maintain user preferences.
30.  Uninstall shortcutsThis permission allowsRamsonTechtodelete the shortcuts created byRamsonTechin other Android launchers.
31.  Draw over other appsThis permission allows theRamsonTechFreeSwipe tab to be displayed as a floating window on other apps.
32.  Read sync settingsThis permission is used inRamsonTechpower’ssave mode, and allowsRamsonTechto recognize whether sync settings areturned on or off.
33.  Toggle sync on and offThis permission is used inRamsonTechpower’ssave mode, and allowsRamsonTechto synchronize with the user's syncsettings.
34.  Expand/collapse status barThis permission is used for the gesture featureofRamsonTechUser System to expand and collapse the status bar.
35.  Install shortcutsThis permission is used to install shortcuts onother launchers, so users can continue to useRamsonTechrelated functions.

How we protect your informationWe have implemented commercially reasonabletechnical and organizational measures to protect your personal information fromaccidental loss and from loss, misuse and unauthorized access, disclosure,alteration and destruction. However, please note that although we takereasonable measures to protect your information, no app, website, Internettransmission, computer system or wireless connection is completely secure.

Sensitive InformationWe ask that you not send us and also not discloseany sensitive Personal Information (e.g., information related to politicalopinions, racial or ethnic origin, religion or other beliefs, health etc) on orthrough application, the Services or the Site or otherwise to us. Keep safeyour personal and sensitive information and strictly we do not ask to submit.

How we share your informationWe may retain third parties to perform serviceson our behalf and we may collaborate with third parties with respect toparticular products or services. These third parties may be provided withaccess to the information needed to perform their functions, as long as theyagree to keep the information confidential. We reserve the right to discloseyour information as required by law, when we believe disclosure is necessary to
comply with a regulatory requirement, judicial proceeding, court order, orlegal process served on us, or to protect the safety, rights, or property ofour users, the publicRamsonTech.
Children's PrivacyWe are committed to protecting the privacy ofchildren. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children underthe age of 13.

Changes to the Privacy PolicyRamsonTechmay, in its sole discretion, change this PrivacyPolicy from time to time, any and all changes will be reflected here and thedate new versions are posted will be stated at the top of this Privacy Policy,so please review it periodically.
